manual_step_categories.description: Verify branch, head, status, and recent log before changing R16 artifacts.
Run bounded validators and tests instead of historical full sweeps during implementation passes.
Account for untracked files because git diff --stat and --numstat omit them.
Regenerate only after validation proves deterministic state artifacts are stale.
Recover from compaction or restart using exact repo-backed refs rather than raw chat history.
Keep proof/status/regeneration separate from validation/commit/push when context is high.
Prefer direct command invocation and targeted Select-String/field extraction when shell wrappers are brittle.

observed_failure_messages: Error running remote compact task: stream disconnected before completion.
compaction_failure_metrics.main_occurrence_window: long finalization passes after proof/status/regeneration work and before commit/push

deterministic_drift_metrics.drift_source: status and proof edits changed files in the context-load plan

stale_ref_findings.description: Deterministic byte and line drift in status/proof surfaces can make accepted generated artifacts stale until regenerated in order.
git diff --stat and git diff --numstat do not include untracked files, so explicit untracked line counting must be preserved.

fixture_bloat_metrics.mitigation: one full valid fixture plus compact invalid mutation specs

local_wip_interruption_metrics.interruption_pattern: compaction failures during long finalization passes before commit/push
local_wip_interruption_metrics.mitigation_recommendation: split finalization into B1 proof/status/regeneration and B2 validation/commit/push

process_friction_findings.summary: Repeated automatic compaction failures created local-WIP recovery cycles.
R16-019 initially produced around 150k+ unstaged/added lines from full-copy invalid fixtures; compact mutation specs mitigated it.
git diff --stat and git diff --numstat omit untracked files, requiring explicit untracked line counting.
Status and proof edits changed deterministic byte/line inputs and cascaded through R16-016, R16-017, and R16-019 through R16-024.
Each newly authorized task artifact required narrow validator allowlist updates while keeping future tasks forbidden.
A/B split helped, but finalization pass B was still too large when proof, status, regeneration, validation, commit, and push were combined.
rg.exe access denial, wrapper interpolation, array invocation, and short timeouts created PowerShell/tooling friction.
Large generated state JSON files add context pressure and should be inspected through validators or targeted field extraction.
The context guard is correctly failed closed and should be treated as an expected signal, not a failed task.
R16 does not claim runtime memory, retrieval/vector runtime, product runtime, autonomous agents, executable handoffs/transitions, or solved Codex reliability.

next_milestone_planning_implications.recommendation: Use B1 for proof/status/regeneration only and B2 for validation/commit/push only.
Keep one full valid fixture and compact invalid mutation specs; construct invalid objects in memory.
Always pair git diff --stat and --numstat with explicit untracked line counting before validation.
Prefer validators, Select-String, and targeted JSON field extraction over opening large generated JSON.
Treat the failed-closed guard as proof the context surface remains too large and that the guard works.
Authorize the current task narrowly while keeping R16-026 and later work forbidden until explicitly started.

current_posture.active_through_scope: R16-025 bounded friction metrics report only

non_claims: R16-025 is a bounded friction metrics report only.
Operator-observed process evidence is not machine proof.
Raw chat history is not canonical evidence.
Broad/full repo scan is not used.
Exact provider tokenization and billing are not claimed.
No final R16 audit acceptance is claimed.
No final proof package completion is claimed.
No vector search runtime is claimed.
No solved Codex compaction is claimed.
No solved Codex reliability is claimed.
R16-027 or later is not claimed.
R13 remains failed/partial and not closed.
